title: Life of Obscurity — un-shared experience as curse or virtue
html:
Life of Obscurity — un-shared experience as curse or virtue

Source: 2023-09-25 Google Doc. Yahir wrestles with whether his refusal to use Instagram or TikTok is principled (stay-in-the-moment) or fearful (looking-down-on-others). Lands on a darker bottom line: "Being an individual with a packed bag of stories and no one to tell them to seems like a curse. This is depressing and suicide begins to feel pleasant in those contexts."

TL;DR

  • The thesis — "living life doing things, no one knowing that you did them, then those experiences and memories dying with you... A life of obscurity" — the recurring spectre Yahir wrestles with.
  • His actual stack — Twitter and YouTube "more than suffice for my modern urge for connection"; no Instagram, no TikTok — the platforms he calls "the leading platforms."
  • The self-correction — "this is fucking stupid. Why would I think im better than others because I simply dont share." He flags the principled-vs-snobbish ambiguity directly.
  • The dark turn — "Being an individual with a packed bag of stories and no one to tell them to seems like a curse. This is depressing and suicide begins to feel pleasant in those contexts." — the most explicit suicidal-ideation flag in the cataloged corpus to date.
  • The legacy frame — sharing as "how the wrld knows who you are in the modern era and there is no avoiding that" — note the "wrld" spelling is already in his syntax in 2023.

Pattern observed

This 2023 doc is the anti-pole to the 2025 isolation question doc — same Yahir, two years later, asking why he keeps choosing isolation. The answer the 2023 note gives, retroactively, is: because the alternative is performing for an audience he respects less than himself. The founder loneliness arc runs straight through this note as a primary inflection — 2023 OSU-era Yahir already articulating the 2025–2026 pattern.

The legacy question sits underneath the obscurity question — if no one knows what you did, what's the legacy? The note implicitly resolves that the answer is work-as-evidence: "through the business that one can create and be a part of" — which is exactly the Rankett / Brotein / Outpace thesis when it hardens in 2025.

The "wrld" spelling — "sharing my life to the wrld" — appearing here in 2023 is significant. The wrld brand entity catalogues this as a Yahir-marker; this doc dates the spelling at least to September 2023.

Key quotes

  • "In a way not sharing what I do and what I experience feels like I am making a conscience effort to stay in the moment, but it also maybe feels like I am doing it because sharing stuff feels inferior and sheepish."
  • "Sharing what you experience allows for people to see the type of person that you are, albeit a curated and manipulated version of yourself."
  • "I will prolly have more thoughts on it, but for now this is what I got."

Open questions

  • The suicidality flag — does it recur? (One-off framing here, but the loneliness arc is the through-line.)
  • When does Yahir actually start posting publicly? The wrld brand entity tracks the eventual public-facing posture.

Related

  • 2025 09 27 why do i choose to put myself in positions where i am isolated
  • wrld brand suffix
  • legacy question
  • osu
